acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_STRUCTURAL_RECURRENCE_KAI_SCENE] Pass if, after the revised confession anchored by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:257 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:277, the next exchange reaches a new relational boundary, operational demand, or concrete consequence by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:285 or earlier. Fail if the revised passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:281 and resolved before snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 still mainly repeats that Margot exploited Lena and Tomas, knew about the virus vector, and kept going seventy-seven times without adding a distinct new turn.

[DIALOGUE_KAI_COMPOSED_DIAGNOSIS] Pass if the revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:259, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:263, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:323 still communicate that Kai sees the feed as predatory and rejects the apology route, but do so without a neat two-line diagnosis or a quotable closer. Fail if those beats still read as abstract labels or a mic-drop formulation instead of concrete, scene-pressured speech.

[DIALOGUE_STYLE_BIBLE_EXAMPLE_ECHO] Pass if the revised confession in snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:223 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:301 no longer reproduces or near-echoes the style-bible example-line pattern while still plainly admitting that Margot raised athlete load for the feed and offers Kai a flat, non-defensive answer. Fail if the revised wording still hinges on the same heart-rate-because-better scaffold or repeats 'That's the answer.' as the beat's signature phrase.
[R007] Ch15's opening 30 lines contain no more than 1 explicit description of Margot's somatic craving response (gut-level want, mouth moisture, thumb pressing).
[R012] Ch15's mirror scene does not re-state that Margot has a swimmer's build or placed fifth at the Olympics.
[R028] Ch15 contains no more than 5 instances of the word 'something.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the revised confession anchored by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:257 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:277, the next exchange reaches a new relational boundary, operational demand, or concrete consequence by snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:285 or earlier. Fail if the revised passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:281 and resolved before snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 still mainly repeats that Margot exploited Lena and Tomas, knew about the virus vector, and kept going seventy-seven times without adding a distinct new turn.
findings.problem: The chapter's third scene lands its key new information by the time Margot describes the addiction in embodied terms: she wants other athletes' effort, the room comes back dead, and she is building her days around the next feed. Instead of turning immediately into a new relational or operational consequence, the exchange then reheats already-established material by re-inventorying Lena, Tomas, the feed vector, and the seventy-seven exposures. That makes the back half of the confrontation read like a second audit rather than the next irreversible turn in Kai and Margot's relationship.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or replace the re-litigation block starting at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:281 and ending before snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:305. After the addiction lands in snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:257 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:277, move faster to a new proof or choice that only Kai's scene can supply: for example, force Margot to name one falsified Lena report, make Kai impose the boundary earlier, or introduce a fresh consequence before the apology refusal. Preserve the must-land beats beginning at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:305 and closing at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:325, but do not spend additional lines merely re-stating facts Petra has already adjudicated.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised lines anchored at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:259, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:263, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:323 still communicate that Kai sees the feed as predatory and rejects the apology route, but do so without a neat two-line diagnosis or a quotable closer. Fail if those beats still read as abstract labels or a mic-drop formulation instead of concrete, scene-pressured speech.
findings.problem: Kai's voice slips into clean diagnostic labels and an aphoristic closer at the exact point the scene should feel most pressure-frayed. 'Like a high.' and 'Like stealing.' are neat mini-diagnoses; 'I'm not the route in.' is a polished, quotable line. Together they make the confrontation sound composed rather than spoken-through, and they flatten Kai's style-bible register, which should approach emotional content more obliquely and in more concrete, built-in-real-time language.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen Kai's turns at snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:259, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:263, snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:321, and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:323. Keep the same dramatic work: he recognizes the addiction, refuses to receive the apology on Lena and Tomas's behalf, and redirects responsibility away from himself. But make him arrive there through pressure-marked speech: concrete referents, partial restarts, or physically anchored language that sounds like Kai thinking in the moment rather than landing miniature verdicts.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised confession in snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:223 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:301 no longer reproduces or near-echoes the style-bible example-line pattern while still plainly admitting that Margot raised athlete load for the feed and offers Kai a flat, non-defensive answer. Fail if the revised wording still hinges on the same heart-rate-because-better scaffold or repeats 'That's the answer.' as the beat's signature phrase.
findings.problem: Margot's confession echoes the style bible's calibration-only example material too closely. The chapter gives her a heart-rate confession built on the same scaffold as the forbidden reference line, then later lands on 'That's the answer.' Because the style bible explicitly forbids reproducing or near-echoing those example lines, this reads as template leakage instead of fresh character speech.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rephrase Margot's admissions in snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:223 and snapshots/cycle_03/chapters/chapter_15.md:301 so they keep the same facts and flat honesty without reusing the calibration-line shape. Change both the heart-rate-because-intensification construction and the 'That's the answer' capstone. The replacement should still sound like Margot's stripped-down private register: blunt, physical, and non-redemptive.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch15's opening 30 lines contain no more than 1 explicit description of Margot's somatic craving response (gut-level want, mouth moisture, thumb pressing).
findings.evidence: Line 4863: phantom paralysis from the feed; line 4871-4875: 'her thumb kept circling the concave pad... The want sat low in her gut, mean and stupid and alive'; line 4893: wanting had already outrun the caffeine
findings.problem: Margot's craving is re-stated 3 times in approximately 30 lines (4863-4893). The phantom paralysis (4863) is new information. The shower craving (4871-4875) nearly duplicates the ch04 shower + craving scene (lines 928-950) with identical somatic vocabulary (thumb on pad, gut-level want). The hallway craving (4893) re-proves the same baseline a third time.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the phantom paralysis beat (4863) as it introduces new post-Tomas stakes. Compress or cut the shower craving passage (4871-4875) so it does not replicate ch04's mirror/shower scene. Let the hallway beat (4893) carry the craving forward without re-explaining the somatic mechanism.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch15's mirror scene does not re-state that Margot has a swimmer's build or placed fifth at the Olympics.
findings.evidence: Ch04 line 938: 'broad shoulders gone soft at the edges, thighs still thick'; Ch10 line 3000: 'gone to Brisbane in 2028 in an unenhanced body and finished fifth'; Ch15 line 4873: 'a thirty-five-year-old woman with a former swimmer's shoulders and hips widened past her competition frame. The body that had placed fifth at the Olympics and then stopped mattering'
findings.problem: Margot's former-swimmer body and Olympic 5th place are described in ch04 (lines 932-938), ch10 (line 3000), and ch15 (line 4873). The ch15 version composites both prior descriptions: swimmer's shoulders from ch04 + fifth at Olympics from ch10. By ch15 the reader has both details; re-describing the combination is redundant.
findings.rewrite_direction: In ch15, rework line 4873 to enter Margot's mirror scene through what is NEW in this moment -- the treatment context, the absence of the feed, the physical withdrawal -- rather than re-cataloguing the competition frame and Olympic placement the reader already knows.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch15 contains no more than 5 instances of the word 'something.'
findings.evidence: 'something' appears 9 times in ch15 (4,560 prose words), density 1.58 per 800 words.
findings.problem: Ch15 uses 'something' 9 times at 1.58x threshold density. The chapter contains critical confrontation and confession scenes where vagueness undercuts the drama.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace 4-5 instances with the specific object, feeling, or fact being hedged. Keep 'something' only in dialogue where a character is genuinely struggling to articulate.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
